projections_eos.weakness: No frontend, v1 CRM schema may be outdated

saas.description: 30 files — TypeScript Hono backend (v2 events schema), unified API entrypoint
saas.strength: Serves as unified API server, 14 EOS tables, 12 route files
saas.weakness: No frontend, severe schema drift (7 orphan tables, broken migration journal), no deployment config

windows_beast_dev.description: 603 files — React+Express+Drizzle full-stack app, Clerk auth, AI gateway (5 providers), CRM + org chart + task board + workflows
windows_beast_dev.strength: MOST COMPLETE standalone EOS app — full frontend, full backend, full AI integration, full feature set
windows_beast_dev.weakness: On feature/company-system branch (not main), Clerk auth incompatible with UMH platform auth, monolith schema not separated

github_entrepreneuros.description: Full-stack TypeScript app — client/ + server/ + shared/ + Drizzle + Vite + Tailwind
github_entrepreneuros.strength: Original full EOS app with frontend, dormant since April but may be the most complete source
github_entrepreneuros.weakness: Dormant since 2026-04-16, development moved to OS/saas/, Replit origin (may have Replit-specific code)

EOS.strongest_runtime_operational_candidate: saas/ API + projections/eos/ agents running on VPS
known_stale_sources: saas/ schema drift — 7 orphan migration tables
projections/eos/ v1 CRM schema vs saas/ v2 events schema
EOS.confidence: high — all sources now inspected, but operator decision required due to auth/schema divergence
evidence: Schema v1/v2 split across projections/eos and saas/
saas/ is unified API entrypoint, not just EOS projection
CRM tables have text org_id breaking RLS
7 tables exist only in migrations not schema.ts
4 tables in schema.ts with no migration
EOS.required_operator_decision: FOUR sources fully mapped. Beast is most complete standalone app but uses Clerk (not UMH auth). VPS saas/ is UMH-integrated but backend-only. Key decision: migrate Beast frontend to UMH auth, or rebuild frontend on VPS?
EOS.required_next_inspection: Windows Beast /dev EOS app comparison; GitHub EOS repo comparison

summary: UMH is canonical on VPS. EOS has 3 competing sources with low confidence — operator decision required after Beast/GitHub inspection. CreatorOS and LyfeOS have no visible full source — very low confidence. Shared platform infra is canonical in substrate/ and transports/.
